name: Support Vector Machine (SVM)
applicable_scenarios: margin-based binary classification on small-to-medium data
non-linear separation via kernel functions (RBF, polynomial)
python_template:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.svm import SVC, SVR

def fit_svc(X, y, C=1.0, kernel='rbf', gamma='scale'):
    model = SVC(C=C, kernel=kernel, gamma=gamma, probability=True)
    model.fit(X, y)
    return model

def fit_svr(X, y, C=1.0, epsilon=0.1, kernel='rbf'):
    model = SVR(C=C, epsilon=epsilon, kernel=kernel)
    model.fit(X, y)
    return model
typical_cases: handwritten digit classification with modest n
text classification with sparse features + linear kernel
bioinformatics classification on small gene-expression datasets
common_pitfalls: O(n^2)-O(n^3) training; doesn't scale past ~100k samples without approximation
requires feature scaling, especially for RBF
probability estimates require extra Platt scaling (slow, noisier)
multi-class is emulated via one-vs-rest — large K gets expensive
